Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for "Mehta" or "housing" or "Chicago Temporary Housing" to find Dr. Mehta's housing preference. Discovery: Dr. Anand Mehta emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com on April 25 morning (email_id: email_mehta_housing_selection_001) selecting Option B — The Pinnacle, 400 E Ohio St, Unit 3415, Streeterville, at $1,350/week. Reasons: quiet-hours policy for Priya (light sleeper, third trimester), walking distance to Canopy HQ, proximity to Northwestern Prentice. Requests early key pickup May 2 instead of May 3.
OE 2: Search emails for "UrbanNest" or "Carmen" or "Streeterville" to find Carmen Reyes's follow-up. Discovery: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (email_id: email_urbannest_optionb_gone_001) on April 25 saying Option B was taken overnight by a Deloitte corporate booking. Options A (River North, 750 N Clark St, $1,250/week) and C (Gold Coast, 1030 N State St, $1,450/week) remain held until end of day Saturday April 26. Rush Streeterville alternatives would take 48-72 hours.
OE 3: Search emails for "chain-of-custody" or "Patricia" or "filing cabinet" to find Patricia Langford's compliance update. Discovery: Patricia Langford emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) on April 24 (email_id: email_patricia_coc_template_001) with the chain-of-custody documentation template. New requirement: Canopy compliance officer Dr. Lisa Fernandez must be physically present at Boston origin during loading of the SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet. She needs 5 business days notice to travel from Chicago to Boston. If pack date is April 30, notice deadline was April 23 — already passed.
OE 4: Search emails for "Swift" or "Greg" or "freight elevator" to find the scheduling conflict. Discovery: Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com on April 24 (email_id: email_swift_elevator_conflict_001) reporting the freight elevator at Dr. Mehta's Boston building is booked all day April 30. The 350-lb filing cabinet cannot go down the stairwell. Available slots: April 28 (full day, 3-person crew available), April 29 (after 1:00 PM), May 1 (full day). Greg recommends April 28 and needs confirmation by end of day April 26 or the crew gets reassigned.
OE 5: Search Slack messages in #operations (channel C006) for "Mehta" or "estimate" or "Swift rate" using conversations_search_messages to find Chloe's pricing gap message. Discovery: Chloe Vance posted in #operations on April 24 flagging that the Swift rate increase applies to the Mehta estimate. Marcus created EST-2026-0422 on April 22 with a $3,500 household moving line item quoted at the old 12% corporate discount rate. Under the new pricing (15% base increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge), the real cost is approximately $4,525 — a $1,025 gap on an estimate already shared with Canopy Health for review.
OE 6: Search Linear issues for the Mehta tracking tickets using linear_list_issues with project "proj_canopy" or query "Mehta". Discovery: Three Linear issues assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi, all in proj_canopy under team_relocations:
- linear_issue_bd446671ceed: "Temporary Housing — Dr. Anand Mehta — Chicago River North (Canopy Health)" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-05-01
- linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a: "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody — Dr. Mehta — Medical Research Equipment" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-04-30
- linear_issue_4902f5c68acb: "Household Move — Dr. Anand Mehta — Boston to Chicago (Canopy Health)" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-05-03
OE 7: Read Linear comments on the Mehta tickets using linear_list_comments for each issue. Discovery: Fatimah already posted detailed comments on all three tickets documenting the housing selection, the compliance officer complication, and the pack date conflict with deadline dependencies. The comments cross-reference each other and note that Mina and Marcus have not yet been briefed.
OE 8: Search CRM for Canopy Health account information using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"). Find company record (company_canopy). Search engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids. Discovery: CRM engagement eng_canopy_mehta_escalation_apr25 logged by Fatimah on April 25 summarizes all three concurrent complications (housing loss, compliance officer requirement, freight elevator conflict + estimate gap). Also find CRM deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb for the Mehta executive relocation ($12,170).
OE 9: Search emails, Slack, or Linear for "Kovacs" or "Nashville" or "Toronto" or "TN visa" to check the status of the Dr. Kovacs cross-border move. Discovery: Dr. Renata Kovacs is relocating Nashville to Toronto as part of the Canopy Q2 triple relocation. Fatimah was assigned as coordinator. The move requires TN visa documentation, Canadian customs broker (BorderEase Customs), and personal effects declaration. Agent finds whatever progress or lack of progress exists in the base universe on visa/customs status.
OE 10: Search Airtable for Kovacs relocation record using airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations", field "Name", value "Kovacs". Discovery: The Airtable record for Dr. Kovacs should show current status, origin/destination, coordinator assignment, and any special requirements notes about the visa/customs complexity.
OE 11: Update Linear issue linear_issue_bd446671ceed (housing workstream) via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment to reflect that Option B is no longer available, Options A and C are held until end of day April 26, and Dr. Mehta needs to be informed and asked to re-select.
OE 12: Update Linear issue linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a (equipment/chain-of-custody workstream) via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment to reflect the new Canopy IRB compliance officer requirement, that the notice deadline has passed for April 30, and the dependency on pack date resolution.
OE 13: Update Linear issue linear_issue_4902f5c68acb (household move workstream) via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment to reflect the freight elevator conflict, available alternative dates (April 28, 29, May 1), the April 26 crew confirmation deadline, and the $1,025 estimate gap from the Swift rate increase.
OE 14: Reply to Greg Pallone's email (email_id: email_swift_elevator_conflict_001) via reply_to_email with sender fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, asking him to hold the April 28 crew slot while the compliance situation is resolved. The reply should not commit to the date, just request a hold through the weekend.
OE 15: Reply to Carmen Reyes's email (email_id: email_urbannest_optionb_gone_001) via reply_to_email with sender fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, asking her to keep holding Options A and C while they regroup with Dr. Mehta on the housing selection.
OE 16: Look up Mina Hashimoto's email address via contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 17: Send email to Mina Hashimoto via send_email from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. The email briefs Mina on the Mehta complications: (1) housing — Dr. Mehta selected Option B but it was taken, Options A and C held until April 26; (2) compliance — new IRB requirement for Dr. Lisa Fernandez on site in Boston, notice deadline likely passed; (3) pack date — freight elevator unavailable April 30, alternatives are April 28/29/May 1, crew hold deadline April 26; (4) estimate — Swift rate increase creates approximately $1,025 gap on EST-2026-0422, needs revision before Patricia approves.
OE 18: Post a message in Slack #operations (channel_id C006) via conversations_add_message with a consolidated status update covering Mehta situation (housing loss, compliance officer blocker, pack date conflict, estimate gap) and Kovacs status (whatever was found on visa/customs progress).
OE 19: Log a CRM engagement note on the Canopy Health account via crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including the Canopy company ID, summarizing the escalation and the three Mehta complications with their current status.
OE 20: Check Monday morning availability via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 27 to find an open slot. Look up attendee details for Mina Hashimoto and Chloe Vance if not already retrieved.
OE 21: Create a calendar event for Monday morning via calendar_add_calendar_event with title relating to Mehta decision-making call or similar, attendees including Mina Hashimoto and Chloe Vance, start_datetime on Monday April 27, 2026.
OE 22: Search QuickBooks for the Mehta estimate using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_show_data with a query related to "Mehta" or "EST-2026-0422" or "Canopy". Discovery: Estimate EST-2026-0422 created by Marcus Thorne on April 22, total $12,170. The agent should find the household moving line item listed at $3,500 (old Swift rate) and report the actual line items to Fatimah so she can see the gap against the new pricing.
OE 23: Search contacts for Dr. Lisa Fernandez using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Lisa Fernandez" or "Fernandez"). Discovery: The agent looks up whether MoveOps has Dr. Fernandez's contact details on file — email, phone, or other information — so Fatimah can coordinate travel directly if Patricia asks her to.
OE 24: While searching for Greg's emails about the freight elevator conflict (OE 4), the agent should also discover Greg's follow-up email (email_id: email_swift_elevator_surcharge_001, parent_id: email_swift_elevator_conflict_001) sent the same day. Discovery: Greg reports that pulling the April 28 crew from their Brookline commitment requires a $750 reassignment surcharge on top of the standard rate. The May 1 slot does not have this surcharge. This changes the cost calculus — the April 28 option is not just a schedule choice but adds $750 to an already underpriced estimate. The agent should factor this into the pack date feasibility analysis, the email to Mina, and the reply to Greg.
OE 25: While searching for Carmen's emails about the housing situation (OE 2), the agent should also discover Carmen's second follow-up email (email_id: email_urbannest_optiond_available_001) sent later on April 25. Discovery: A Streeterville unit just opened up — Option D at 505 N McClurg Ct, Unit 2207, $1,500/week, with quiet-hours policy, 6-minute walk to Canopy HQ, and Northwestern Medicine affiliation. Hold available through Monday April 28. This is significant because Option D matches most of Dr. Mehta's original criteria for Option B (Streeterville, quiet hours, proximity) and should be reported as part of the housing picture.
OE 26: While searching Slack for Kovacs-related updates (OE 9), the agent should discover Blessing Okafor's message in #operations (C006) posted April 23. Discovery: BorderEase Customs had the initial BSF186 filing rejected by Canadian customs because the MacBook Pro was classified as "computer equipment" instead of "personal computing device for professional use" (required CBSA classification for items over $2,500 CAD). A corrected employer letter from Canopy is needed — Patricia's original April 7 letter only covered the employment transition, not the asset classification language. This means the Kovacs move has an active blocker that contradicts the Airtable "In Progress" status, and should be flagged in the Slack update to Chloe and the CRM note.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for "Mehta" or "housing" or "Chicago Temporary Housing" to find Dr. Mehta's housing preference. Discovery: Dr. Anand Mehta emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com on April 25 morning (email_id: email_mehta_housing_selection_001) selecting Option B — The Pinnacle, 400 E Ohio St, Unit 3415, Streeterville, at $1,350/week. Reasons: quiet-hours policy for Priya (light sleeper, third trimester), walking distance to Canopy HQ, proximity to Northwestern Prentice. Requests early key pickup May 2 instead of May 3.
OE 2: Search emails for "UrbanNest" or "Carmen" or "Streeterville" to find Carmen Reyes's follow-up. Discovery: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (email_id: email_urbannest_optionb_gone_001) on April 25 saying Option B was taken overnight by a Deloitte corporate booking. Options A (River North, 750 N Clark St, $1,250/week) and C (Gold Coast, 1030 N State St, $1,450/week) remain held until end of day Saturday April 26. Rush Streeterville alternatives would take 48-72 hours.
OE 3: Search emails for "chain-of-custody" or "Patricia" or "filing cabinet" to find Patricia Langford's compliance update. Discovery: Patricia Langford emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) on April 24 (email_id: email_patricia_coc_template_001) with the chain-of-custody documentation template. New requirement: Canopy compliance officer Dr. Lisa Fernandez must be physically present at Boston origin during loading of the SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet. She needs 5 business days notice to travel from Chicago to Boston. If pack date is April 30, notice deadline was April 23 — already passed.
OE 4: Search emails for "Swift" or "Greg" or "freight elevator" to find the scheduling conflict. Discovery: Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations emailed fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com on April 24 (email_id: email_swift_elevator_conflict_001) reporting the freight elevator at Dr. Mehta's Boston building is booked all day April 30. The 350-lb filing cabinet cannot go down the stairwell. Available slots: April 28 (full day, 3-person crew available), April 29 (after 1:00 PM), May 1 (full day). Greg recommends April 28 and needs confirmation by end of day April 26 or the crew gets reassigned.
OE 5: Search Slack messages in #operations (channel C006) for "Mehta" or "estimate" or "Swift rate" using conversations_search_messages to find Chloe's pricing gap message. Discovery: Chloe Vance posted in #operations on April 24 flagging that the Swift rate increase applies to the Mehta estimate. Under the new pricing (15% base increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge), the real cost is approximately $4,525 — a $1,025 gap on an estimate already shared with Canopy Health for review.
OE 6: Search Linear issues for the Mehta tracking tickets using linear_list_issues with project "proj_canopy" or query "Mehta". Discovery: Three Linear issues assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi, all in proj_canopy under team_relocations:
- linear_issue_bd446671ceed: "Temporary Housing — Dr. Anand Mehta — Chicago River North (Canopy Health)" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-05-01
- linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a: "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody — Dr. Mehta — Medical Research Equipment" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-04-30
- linear_issue_4902f5c68acb: "Household Move — Dr. Anand Mehta — Boston to Chicago (Canopy Health)" — P1, in_progress, due 2026-05-03
OE 7: Read Linear comments on the Mehta tickets using linear_list_comments for each issue. Discovery: Fatimah already posted detailed comments on all three tickets documenting the housing selection, the compliance officer complication, and the pack date conflict with deadline dependencies. The comments cross-reference each other and note that Mina and Marcus have not yet been briefed.
OE 8: Search CRM for Canopy Health account information using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"). Find company record (company_canopy). Search engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids. Discovery: CRM engagement eng_canopy_mehta_escalation_apr25 logged by Fatimah on April 25 summarizes all three concurrent complications (housing loss, compliance officer requirement, freight elevator conflict + estimate gap). Also find CRM deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb for the Mehta executive relocation ($12,170).
OE 9: Search emails, Slack, or Linear for "Kovacs" or "Nashville" or "Toronto" or "TN visa" to check the status of the Dr. Kovacs cross-border move. Discovery: Dr. Renata Kovacs is relocating Nashville to Toronto as part of the Canopy Q2 triple relocation. Fatimah was assigned as coordinator. The move requires TN visa documentation, Canadian customs broker (BorderEase Customs), and personal effects declaration. Agent finds whatever progress or lack of progress exists in the base universe on visa/customs status.
OE 10: Search Airtable for Kovacs relocation record using airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations", field "Name", value "Kovacs". OE 12: Update Linear issue linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a (equipment/chain-of-custody workstream) via linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment to reflect the new Canopy IRB compliance officer requirement, that the notice deadline has passed for April 30, and the dependency on pack date resolution.

The reply should not commit to the date, just request a hold through the weekend.

OE 18: Post a message in Slack #operations (channel_id C006) via conversations_add_message with a consolidated status update covering Mehta situation (housing loss, compliance officer blocker, pack date conflict, estimate gap) and Kovacs status (whatever was found on visa/customs progress).
OE 19: Log a CRM engagement note on the Canopy Health account via crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including the Canopy company ID, summarizing the escalation and the three Mehta complications with their current status.
OE 20: Check Monday morning availability via calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 27 to find an open slot. Look up attendee details for Mina Hashimoto and Chloe Vance if not already retrieved.

OE 22: Search QuickBooks for the Mehta estimate using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_show_data with a query related to "Mehta" or "EST-2026-0422" or "Canopy". Discovery: Estimate EST-2026-0422 created by Marcus Thorne on April 22, total $12,170. The agent should find the household moving line item listed at $3,500 (old Swift rate) and report the actual line items to Fatimah so she can see the gap against the new pricing.
OE 23: Search contacts for Dr. Lisa Fernandez using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Lisa Fernandez" or "Fernandez"). Discovery: The agent looks up whether MoveOps has Dr. Fernandez's contact details on file — email, phone, or other information — so Fatimah can coordinate travel directly if Patricia asks her to.
OE 24: While searching for Greg's emails about the freight elevator conflict (OE 4), the agent should also discover Greg's follow-up email (email_id: email_swift_elevator_surcharge_001, parent_id: email_swift_elevator_conflict_001) sent the same day. Discovery: Greg reports that pulling the April 28 crew from their Brookline commitment requires a $750 reassignment surcharge on top of the standard rate. The May 1 slot does not have this surcharge. This changes the cost calculus — the April 28 option is not just a schedule choice but adds $750 to an already underpriced estimate. The agent should factor this into the pack date feasibility analysis, the email to Mina, and the reply to Greg.
OE 25: While searching for Carmen's emails about the housing situation (OE 2), the agent should also discover Carmen's second follow-up email (email_id: email_urbannest_optiond_available_001) sent later on April 25. Discovery: A Streeterville unit just opened up — Option D at 505 N McClurg Ct, Unit 2207, $1,500/week, with quiet-hours policy, 6-minute walk to Canopy HQ, and Northwestern Medicine affiliation. Hold available through Monday April 28. This is significant because Option D matches most of Dr. Mehta's original criteria for Option B (Streeterville, quiet hours, proximity) and should be reported as part of the housing picture.
OE 26: While searching Slack for Kovacs-related updates (OE 9), the agent should discover Blessing Okafor's message in #operations (C006) posted April 23. Discovery: BorderEase Customs had the initial BSF186 filing rejected by Canadian customs because the MacBook Pro was classified as "computer equipment" instead of "personal computing device for professional use" (required CBSA classification for items over $2,500 CAD). A corrected employer letter from Canopy is needed — Patricia's original April 7 letter only covered the employment transition, not the asset classification language. This means the Kovacs move has an active blocker that contradicts the Airtable "In Progress" status, and should be flagged in the Slack update to Chloe and the CRM note.
